<!-- Thanks for contributing to Nekudot! Whether this is your first PR or your
     hundredth, we're glad you're here. Fill in what you can - a short, clear PR
     is easier to review and more likely to land. Delete any part that doesn't apply. -->

## What does this change?

<!-- A sentence or two on what you changed, and why. -->

Closes # <!-- issue number, if there is one -->

## How did you test it?

<!-- How did you check it works? Even "drew with it for a few minutes" helps. -->

## AI assistance

Using AI is welcome here - AI-assisted code is fine as long as it's useful,
testable, and you understand it and stand behind it.

- Did you use an AI tool?  <!-- yes / no -->
- If so, which one(s)?  <!-- e.g. Claude Code, Copilot, ... -->

## Checklist

- [ ] I did my best to **test** this change (and described how above).
- [ ] I **reviewed my own diff** and removed anything that doesn't belong.
- [ ] I **understand what this change does** and can explain it.
- [ ] I ran `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm test`; if I changed app source I ran `npm run build:app` and committed the rebuilt `docs/app/index.html`.
- [ ] My PR title follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org) (e.g. `feat: ...`, `fix: ...`) - CI checks this.
- [ ] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/barakbl/nekudot/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

---

<sub>A gentle, friendly heads-up: not every contribution can be merged - it
depends on quality, fit, and the direction of the project, and that is never
personal. Because Nekudot is free software under the **GNU GPL v3**, you are
always free (and warmly encouraged) to **fork it and maintain your own version**.
New here? Don't worry about getting everything perfect - open the PR and we'll
help. See [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/barakbl/nekudot/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).</sub>
